2. Add config entry (default_model + dimensions)

- dimensions() matches embedding length

- Compare against baseline metrics

- Model selection works via config + CLI
- No regression in lexical search

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u71u","title":"A6.3: Alternating color stripes for results","description":"Add alternating background colors to search results for better visual separation.","notes":"**Goal**: Zebra-striping like spreadsheets - alternating subtle background colors.

**Implementation in result rendering** (tui.rs ~1772):
```rust
for (idx, hit) in pane.hits.iter().enumerate() {
    let is_odd = idx % 2 == 1;
    let stripe_bg = if is_odd { palette.stripe_odd } else { palette.stripe_even };
    
    // Apply stripe_bg to all lines in this result
    let base_style = Style::default().bg(stripe_bg);
    // ... build lines with base_style ...
}
```

**Add to ThemePalette**:
```rust
pub stripe_even: Color,  // e.g., palette.bg (normal)
pub stripe_odd: Color,   // e.g., slightly lighter/darker
```

**Stripe colors**:
- Dark theme: even=#1a1b26, odd=#1e2030
- Light theme: even=#f0f0f5, odd=#e8e8f0

**Files to modify**:
- src/ui/components/theme.rs: add stripe colors
- src/ui/tui.rs: apply alternating bg in result list

**Testing**:
- Verify alternating colors visible
- Test with selected item (selection should override stripe)
- Test both themes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-28T22:00:00Z","updated_at":"2025-11-29T22:12:59.696429368Z","closed_at":"2025-11-29T22:12:59.696429368Z","external_ref":"bd-51f8","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1u71u","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-sux.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:43:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1upf8","title":"Content Truncation (--max-content-length)","description":"# Content Truncation (--max-content-length)

## Problem Statement
The `content` and `snippet` fields can contain very long text (10KB+ for detailed conversations). Even when an agent wants content, they often only need the first few hundred characters to understand context.

## Proposed Solution
Add `--max-content-length N` flag to truncate text fields:
```bash
cass search "error" --json --max-content-length 200
```

Output:
```json
{"content": "First 200 chars of content...", "content_truncated": true, ...}
```

## Design Decisions

### Which Fields Are Affected
- `content` - main message content
- `snippet` - highlighted excerpt
- `title` - usually short, but truncate if needed

### Truncation Indicator
Add `_truncated` suffix field when content is truncated:
```json
{"content": "truncated...", "content_truncated": true}
```

### UTF-8 Safety
Truncate at character boundaries, not byte boundaries. Ensure valid UTF-8 output.

### Ellipsis
Append `...` when truncating to indicate incompleteness.

## Implementation
```rust
fn truncate_content(s: &str, max_len: usize) -> (String, bool) {
    if s.chars().count() <= max_len {
        (s.to_string(), false)
    } else {
        let truncated: String = s.chars().take(max_len.saturating_sub(3)).collect();
        (format!("{}...", truncated), true)
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--max-content-length 100` truncates content to ~100 chars
- [ ] Truncated fields have `_truncated: true` sibling field
- [ ] UTF-8 boundaries respected (no broken characters)
- [ ] Works with `--fields` (truncation applied to selected fields)
- [ ] Ellipsis appended to truncated content
- [ ] Test: various Unicode content truncation

## Effort Estimate
Low - 1-2 hours. Simple string truncation with UTF-8 awareness.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234185Z","updated_at":"2025-12-01T01:45:55.590679447Z","closed_at":"2025-12-01T01:45:55.590679447Z","external_ref":"bd-8e0e","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2zxzf","title":"Verify zero dead style tokens — all 24 tokens referenced in rendering code","description":"## What

Verify that ALL semantic style tokens defined in style_system.rs are referenced in rendering code. This is a GATE task — it should only run after the 4 style-wiring tasks complete. Additionally, add an automated test that fails if any style token becomes dead in the future.

## Method

### Manual verification (one-time):
```bash
for token in STYLE_APP_ROOT STYLE_PANE_BASE STYLE_PANE_FOCUSED \
    STYLE_TEXT_PRIMARY STYLE_TEXT_MUTED STYLE_TEXT_SUBTLE \
    STYLE_STATUS_SUCCESS STYLE_STATUS_WARNING STYLE_STATUS_ERROR STYLE_STATUS_INFO \
    STYLE_RESULT_ROW STYLE_RESULT_ROW_ALT STYLE_RESULT_ROW_SELECTED \
    STYLE_ROLE_USER STYLE_ROLE_ASSISTANT STYLE_ROLE_TOOL STYLE_ROLE_SYSTEM \
    STYLE_ROLE_GUTTER_USER STYLE_ROLE_GUTTER_ASSISTANT STYLE_ROLE_GUTTER_TOOL STYLE_ROLE_GUTTER_SYSTEM \
    STYLE_SCORE_HIGH STYLE_SCORE_MID STYLE_SCORE_LOW \
    STYLE_PILL_ACTIVE STYLE_TAB_ACTIVE STYLE_TAB_INACTIVE \
    STYLE_KBD_KEY STYLE_KBD_DESC; do
    count=$(rg -c "$token" src/ui/app.rs 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    echo "$token: $count references"
done
```

### Automated test (permanent):
Add a test to style_system.rs (or a dedicated test file) that:
1. Collects all `pub const STYLE_*` constants via a known list
2. For each, greps src/ui/app.rs (or reads a test-time snapshot) for usage
3. Fails with a descriptive error if any token has 0 usage

## Required Tests

### Unit test (THE deliverable of this task):
1. `test_no_dead_style_tokens` — Programmatic check that every STYLE_* constant defined in style_system.rs is referenced at least once in app.rs rendering code. Implementation options:
   - **Option A (compile-time)**: Have each STYLE_* constant be a `&str`. The test collects all of them into a Vec, then for each, checks a `USED_STYLES` set that rendering code populates. This requires instrumentation.
   - **Option B (source-level)**: The test reads `src/ui/app.rs` as a string (using `include_str\!` or `fs::read_to_string`) and checks that each token name appears. Simple, effective, catches regressions.
   - **Recommended: Option B** — straightforward, no runtime overhead, catches dead tokens at `cargo test` time.

```rust
#[test]
fn test_no_dead_style_tokens() {
    let app_source = include_str\!("app.rs");
    let tokens = [
        "STYLE_APP_ROOT", "STYLE_PANE_BASE", "STYLE_PANE_FOCUSED",
        "STYLE_TEXT_PRIMARY", "STYLE_TEXT_MUTED", "STYLE_TEXT_SUBTLE",
        // ... all 28+ tokens
    ];
    let mut dead = Vec::new();
    for token in &tokens {
        if \!app_source.contains(token) {
            dead.push(*token);
        }
    }
    assert\!(dead.is_empty(), "Dead style tokens found: {:?}", dead);
}
```

2. `test_style_token_count_matches_definitions` — Count STYLE_* constants in style_system.rs, compare to the test's known list. Fails if someone adds a new token without updating the test.

## Considerations
- If a token is used ONLY via a helper function in style_system.rs (like `score_style()` calls `STYLE_SCORE_HIGH`), the grep on app.rs might not find it. Solution: grep across all of `src/ui/` not just app.rs.
- Some tokens may be used in components/*.rs files. Expand the search scope accordingly.
- The test should be deterministic and fast (no I/O beyond include_str\!).
- If the token count changes (new tokens added), the test must be updated. This is intentional — it forces the developer to wire the new token.

## Acceptance
- Manual grep shows 0 dead tokens
- Automated test `test_no_dead_style_tokens` passes
- Automated test `test_style_token_count_matches_definitions` passes
- Tests are added to the codebase and run in CI
- No clippy warnings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:34:11.031084332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:57:02.073612425Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:57:02.073588210Z","close_reason":"Superseded by canonical FTUI bead graph under coding_agent_session_search-2dccg (Tracks A-K) with preserved scope and stronger dependency/test/logging coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2zxzf","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:00.487069992Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2zxzf","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2kz6t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:35:03.649033304Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2zxzf","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2sp58","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:35:04.132808059Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2zxzf","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2wcwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:35:04.405334740Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2zxzf","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-k25j6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:35:03.034965303Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-30qc","title":"T3: E2E Test Logging Enhancement - Comprehensive Structured Logs","description":"# Epic: Enhance E2E Test Logging

## Goal
Ensure all test runners emit comprehensive, structured JSONL logs following the unified schema.

## Current State
- Unified schema exists (test-results/e2e/SCHEMA.md)
- Rust E2eLogger implemented (tests/util/e2e_log.rs)
- Shell logger implemented (scripts/lib/e2e_log.sh)
- Playwright reporter exists (tests/e2e/reporters/jsonl-reporter.ts)

## Gaps
1. Not all Rust integration tests use E2eLogger
2. Phase-level logging inconsistent
3. Error context sometimes missing
4. Performance metrics not always captured
5. Mobile device tests lack detailed logging

## Approach
- Audit all test files for E2eLogger usage
- Add phase markers for multi-step tests
- Include performance timing in all E2E tests
- Enhance error context with stack traces and screenshots

## Deliverables
- All tests emit structured JSONL
- Combined log aggregation works
- Dashboard-ready metrics","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:21:33.780667740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:36:50.849948862Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:36:50.849879453Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-jjal","title":"Add unit and integration tests for setup workflow","description":"# Add unit and integration tests for setup workflow

## What
Comprehensive test coverage for the remote sources setup feature, including
unit tests for individual components, integration tests for the full workflow,
and a manual test plan for interactive elements.

## Why
This feature has many moving parts (SSH, installation, indexing, config writing)
and failure modes. Tests ensure:
1. Individual components work correctly in isolation
2. The orchestration handles edge cases
3. Future changes don't break existing functionality
4. Error handling works as expected

## Test Strategy

### Unit Tests

#### SSH Probing Tests (sources/probe.rs)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_parse_probe_output_with_cass() {
    let output = "cass 0.1.50\nCLAUDE_FOUND\nCODEX_FOUND\n";
    let result = parse_probe_output(output);
    assert!(matches!(result.cass_status, CassStatus::Installed { .. }));
    assert_eq!(result.detected_agents.len(), 2);
}

#[test]
fn test_parse_probe_output_no_cass() {
    let output = "cass: command not found\nCLAUDE_FOUND\n";
    let result = parse_probe_output(output);
    assert!(matches!(result.cass_status, CassStatus::NotFound));
}

#[test]
fn test_probe_unreachable_host() {
    // Mock SSH failure
    let result = probe_host_with_mock("unreachable", MockSshFailure::ConnectionRefused);
    assert!(!result.reachable);
}

#[test]
fn test_parse_resource_info() {
    let output = "DISK_FREE:50G\nMEM_AVAIL:8G\nOS:linux\nARCH:x86_64\n";
    let info = parse_resource_info(output);
    assert_eq!(info.disk_free_gb, 50);
    assert_eq!(info.mem_avail_gb, 8);
}
```

#### Selection Logic Tests (sources/setup.rs)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_filter_already_configured_hosts() {
    let probed = vec![...];
    let existing = HashSet::from(["host1", "host2"]);
    let filtered = filter_selectable_hosts(&probed, &existing);
    assert!(filtered.iter().all(|h| !existing.contains(&h.name)));
}

#[test]
fn test_selection_respects_unreachable() {
    // Unreachable hosts should show but not be selectable
    let hosts = vec![
        HostProbeResult { name: "reachable", reachable: true, .. },
        HostProbeResult { name: "unreachable", reachable: false, .. },
    ];
    let selectable = get_selectable_indices(&hosts);
    assert!(!selectable.contains(&1));
}

#[test]
fn test_search_filter_matches() {
    let hosts = vec!["css", "csd", "trj", "yto"];
    let matches = filter_by_search(&hosts, "cs");
    assert_eq!(matches, vec!["css", "csd"]);
}
```

#### Config Generation Tests (sources/config.rs)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_generate_source_from_probe() {
    let probe = HostProbeResult {
        host_name: "test",
        detected_agents: vec![
            DetectedAgent { agent_type: AgentKind::Claude, .. },
        ],
        ..
    };
    let source = generate_source_config(&probe);
    assert!(source.paths.iter().any(|p| p.contains("claude")));
}

#[test]
fn test_merge_config_no_overwrite() {
    let mut config = SourcesConfig { sources: vec![existing_source] };
    let result = config.merge_source(duplicate_source);
    assert!(matches!(result, MergeResult::AlreadyExists(_)));
}

#[test]
fn test_path_mapping_generation() {
    let probe = HostProbeResult {
        remote_home: Some("/home/ubuntu".into()),
        has_data_projects: true,
        ..
    };
    let mappings = generate_path_mappings(&probe);
    assert!(mappings.iter().any(|m| m.from == "/data/projects"));
}
```

#### Snapshot Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_config_generation_snapshot() {
    let probe = fixture_probe_result("full_server");
    let source = generate_source_config(&probe);
    insta::assert_yaml_snapshot!(source);
}

#[test]
fn test_toml_output_snapshot() {
    let config = fixture_sources_config();
    let toml = config.to_toml_string().unwrap();
    insta::assert_snapshot!(toml);
}
```

### Integration Tests

#### Mock SSH Infrastructure
Create a mock SSH server for testing without real remotes:
```rust
struct MockSshServer {
    responses: HashMap<String, MockResponse>,
}

impl MockSshServer {
    fn new() -> Self { ... }
    fn expect_command(&mut self, cmd: &str, response: MockResponse) { ... }
    fn run(&self) -> TempSshConfig { ... }
}

// Make SSH layer mockable
pub trait SshExecutor: Send + Sync {
    fn execute(&self, host: &str, command: &str) -> Result<String, SshError>;
}

pub struct RealSshExecutor;
pub struct MockSshExecutor { responses: ... }
```

#### Full Workflow Test
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_setup_workflow_happy_path() {
    let mock = MockSshServer::new();
    mock.expect_command("which cass", MockResponse::NotFound);
    mock.expect_command("cargo install", MockResponse::Success);
    mock.expect_command("cass index", MockResponse::Success);
    
    let result = run_setup_workflow(SetupOptions {
        non_interactive: true,
        hosts: Some(vec!["mock-host".into()]),
        ..
    }).await;
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_setup_resumes_from_state() {
    // Create partial state file (simulating Ctrl+C during install)
    let state = SetupState {
        hosts_probed: vec!["host1", "host2"],
        hosts_selected: vec!["host1"],
        install_started: Some("host1"),
        install_completed: vec![],
        ..
    };
    write_state_file(&state).unwrap();
    
    let result = run_setup_workflow(SetupOptions {
        resume: true,
        ..
    }).await;
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    // Verify install was retried, not started from scratch
}
```

#### Error Path Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_setup_handles_ssh_timeout() {
    let mock = MockSshExecutor::with_delay(Duration::from_secs(120));
    let result = run_setup_with_executor(mock, SetupOptions {
        connection_timeout: Duration::from_secs(5),
        ..
    }).await;
    
    assert!(matches!(result.unwrap_err(), SetupError::SshTimeout(_)));
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_setup_handles_install_failure() {
    let mock = MockSshServer::new();
    mock.expect_command("cargo install", MockResponse::Error("compilation failed"));
    
    let result = run_setup_workflow(SetupOptions {
        non_interactive: true,
        hosts: Some(vec!["mock-host".into()]),
        ..
    }).await;
    
    assert!(matches!(result.unwrap_err(), SetupError::InstallFailed(_)));
}
```

### Performance Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_parallel_probing_performance() {
    // Ensure probing 20 hosts completes in reasonable time
    let hosts: Vec<_> = (0..20).map(|i| format!("host{}", i)).collect();
    let start = Instant::now();
    
    let results = probe_hosts_parallel(&hosts, MockSshExecutor::instant());
    
    // With parallelism, should be much faster than 20 * timeout
    assert!(start.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(5));
    assert_eq!(results.len(), 20);
}
```

## Test Fixtures

- `ssh_config/` - Sample SSH config files
  - `basic.config` - Simple host definitions
  - `wildcard.config` - Hosts with wildcards (should be filtered)
  - `many_hosts.config` - 50+ hosts for performance testing
- `probe_outputs/` - Sample probe outputs

  - `cass_not_found.txt`
  - `partial_agents.txt`
  - `resource_info.txt`
- `sources_configs/` - Sample sources.toml configs

  - `existing_sources.toml`
  - `invalid.toml` - For error handling tests
- `mock_responses/` - Canned SSH responses
  - `install_success.txt`
  - `install_failure.txt`
  - `index_progress.txt`

## Manual Test Plan (Interactive Elements)
Since dialoguer interactions can't be fully automated:

### Pre-release checklist:
```
[ ] Fresh setup (no sources.toml)
    [ ] Run `cass sources setup`
    [ ] Verify hosts are discovered from ~/.ssh/config
    [ ] Verify probe status shown correctly
    [ ] Test search filtering (type letters, verify filter)
    [ ] Test select all / deselect all (a / n keys)
    [ ] Test space to toggle, enter to confirm
    [ ] Verify config preview is accurate
    [ ] Test "Edit paths" customization
    [ ] Test "Add custom path" flow
    [ ] Confirm save creates correct sources.toml

[ ] Existing config (some sources already configured)
    [ ] Verify already-configured hosts shown differently
    [ ] Verify skip message for existing sources
    [ ] Verify new sources merge correctly
    [ ] Verify backup created

[ ] Resume capability
    [ ] Start setup, Ctrl+C during probe phase
    [ ] Run setup again with --resume
    [ ] Verify resumes from correct point

[ ] Non-interactive mode
    [ ] Run `cass sources setup --non-interactive --hosts css,csd`
    [ ] Verify no prompts
    [ ] Verify output is machine-parseable

[ ] Error scenarios
    [ ] Test with unreachable host (should show error, continue others)
    [ ] Test with SSH key not loaded (should fail gracefully)
    [ ] Test with no cargo on remote (should suggest manual install)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] >80% code coverage for new modules
- [ ] All error paths have tests
- [ ] Integration test for happy path
- [ ] Integration tests for key failure modes
- [ ] Resume functionality tested
- [ ] Snapshot tests for config generation
- [ ] Performance test for parallel probing
- [ ] SSH layer abstracted for mocking
- [ ] Tests run in CI without real SSH
- [ ] Manual test plan documented and executed

## Dependencies
- Requires: All component tasks to be implemented first

## Considerations
- Testing SSH is inherently tricky (requires mock or real server)
- Consider testcontainers for SSH integration tests in CI
- SSH layer must be mockable via trait (design requirement)
- insta crate for snapshot testing
- Manual test plan should be run before each release

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-jk3m","title":"P5.1: Secret Detection Engine","description":"# P5.1: Secret Detection Scanner

## Goal
Detect sensitive secrets BEFORE encryption and deployment. Use both pattern matching and entropy heuristics to catch API keys, tokens, private keys, and high-entropy blobs.

## Detection Methods

### 1) Pattern Library (regex)
- AWS access/secret keys
- GitHub PAT/OAuth tokens
- OpenAI/Anthropic keys
- JWTs
- Private key headers (PEM)
- Database URLs
- Generic "api_key" style patterns

### 2) Entropy Heuristics
- Identify long base64/hex-like strings
- Compute Shannon entropy and flag above threshold (e.g., > 4.0)
- Apply minimum length (e.g., 20+ chars) to reduce noise

### 3) Scope
Scan:
- Message content
- Conversation titles
- Metadata fields
- Attachment text (when attachments enabled)

## UX / Guardrails
- Show findings grouped by severity (critical/high/medium/low)
- Show sanitized context snippet (avoid leaking full secret)
- Provide allowlist / denylist patterns
- For critical hits: block export unless user explicitly acknowledges
- Robot mode output for CI

## CLI / Wizard Integration
- `cass pages --scan-secrets` (standalone)
- Wizard step shows count and examples
- Optional --fail-on-secrets for CI

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Each built-in regex detects known fixtures
- Entropy detector catches high-entropy strings and ignores normal text
- Allowlist suppresses findings

### Integration Tests
- Export with injected secrets -> scan results include all hits
- Redacted output does not contain secrets

### E2E
- Wizard flow stops on critical secrets and requires confirmation
- JSON output stable for CI parsing

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/pages/secret_scan.rs
- src/pages/wizard.rs (scan step)
- tests/secret_scan.rs

## Exit Criteria
1. Secrets detected reliably with low false positives
2. Entropy-based detection works
3. CI can fail builds on secrets
4. Clear user guidance and logging

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-jk8e","title":"P3.5b: Deep Links & Hash-based Router","description":"# P3.5b: Deep Links & Hash-based Router

## Overview
Implement a hash-based client-side router that enables deep linking to specific conversations, search queries, and application states while maintaining the single-file static site constraint.

## Why Hash-based Routing?
- **Static hosting compatibility**: No server-side routing required
- **Deep linking**: Share links directly to conversations
- **Back/forward navigation**: Browser history integration
- **Bookmarking**: Save links to specific states
- **Session restoration**: Reload page without losing position

## URL Structure

### Route Patterns
```
https://example.github.io/archive/#/                      # Home / conversation list
https://example.github.io/archive/#/search?q=async       # Search results
https://example.github.io/archive/#/c/abc123             # Conversation view
https://example.github.io/archive/#/c/abc123/m/42        # Message within conversation
https://example.github.io/archive/#/settings             # Settings panel
https://example.github.io/archive/#/stats                # Statistics dashboard
```

### Query Parameters
```
#/search?q=query&agent=claude-code&from=2024-01-01
#/c/abc123?highlight=term&scroll=bottom
```

## Router Implementation

### Core Router Class
```javascript
// js/router.js
class HashRouter {
    constructor() {
        this.routes = new Map();
        this.currentRoute = null;
        this.params = {};
        this.queryParams = {};
        
        // Listen to hash changes
        window.addEventListener('hashchange', () => this.handleRoute());
        window.addEventListener('popstate', () => this.handleRoute());
    }
    
    /**
     * Register a route handler
     * @param {string} pattern - Route pattern with :params (e.g., '/c/:id')
     * @param {Function} handler - Handler function(params, queryParams)
     */
    register(pattern, handler) {
        // Convert pattern to regex
        const paramNames = [];
        const regexPattern = pattern.replace(/:(\w+)/g, (_, name) => {
            paramNames.push(name);
            return '([^/]+)';
        });
        
        this.routes.set(pattern, {
            regex: new RegExp(`^${regexPattern}$`),
            paramNames,
            handler
        });
    }
    
    /**
     * Navigate to a route
     * @param {string} path - Path to navigate to
     * @param {Object} options - { replace: boolean, queryParams: {} }
     */
    navigate(path, options = {}) {
        const queryString = options.queryParams 
            ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(options.queryParams).toString()
            : '';
        
        const hash = `#${path}${queryString}`;
        
        if (options.replace) {
            history.replaceState(null, '', hash);
        } else {
            history.pushState(null, '', hash);
        }
        
        this.handleRoute();
    }
    
    /**
     * Handle current route
     */
    handleRoute() {
        const hash = window.location.hash.slice(1) || '/';
        const [path, queryString] = hash.split('?');
        
        // Parse query params
        this.queryParams = queryString 
            ? Object.fromEntries(new URLSearchParams(queryString))
            : {};
        
        // Match route
        for (const [pattern, route] of this.routes) {
            const match = path.match(route.regex);
            if (match) {
                // Extract params
                this.params = {};
                route.paramNames.forEach((name, i) => {
                    this.params[name] = decodeURIComponent(match[i + 1]);
                });
                
                this.currentRoute = pattern;
                route.handler(this.params, this.queryParams);
                return;
            }
        }
        
        // 404 - redirect to home
        this.navigate('/', { replace: true });
    }
    
    /**
     * Go back in history
     */
    back() {
        history.back();
    }
    
    /**
     * Build a URL for a route
     */
    buildUrl(path, queryParams = {}) {
        const queryString = Object.keys(queryParams).length > 0
            ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(queryParams).toString()
            : '';
        return `#${path}${queryString}`;
    }
}

// Singleton instance
export const router = new HashRouter();
```

### Route Registration
```javascript
// js/app.js
import { router } from './router.js';
import { ConversationList } from './views/list.js';
import { ConversationView } from './views/conversation.js';
import { SearchResults } from './views/search.js';
import { Settings } from './views/settings.js';
import { Stats } from './views/stats.js';

// Register routes after app init
function initRoutes() {
    // Home - conversation list
    router.register('/', (params, query) => {
        ConversationList.render({
            filter: query.filter,
            sort: query.sort || 'date',
            page: parseInt(query.page) || 1
        });
    });
    
    // Search results
    router.register('/search', (params, query) => {
        SearchResults.render({
            query: query.q || '',
            agent: query.agent,
            from: query.from,
            to: query.to,
            page: parseInt(query.page) || 1
        });
    });
    
    // Single conversation
    router.register('/c/:id', (params, query) => {
        ConversationView.render({
            conversationId: params.id,
            highlight: query.highlight,
            scrollTo: query.scroll
        });
    });
    
    // Message within conversation
    router.register('/c/:id/m/:messageId', (params, query) => {
        ConversationView.render({
            conversationId: params.id,
            targetMessageId: params.messageId,
            highlight: query.highlight
        });
    });
    
    // Settings
    router.register('/settings', () => Settings.render());
    
    // Statistics
    router.register('/stats', () => Stats.render());
    
    // Handle initial route
    router.handleRoute();
}
```

### Navigation Helper Components
```javascript
// js/components/link.js

/**
 * Create a router-aware link element
 */
export function createLink(path, text, queryParams = {}) {
    const a = document.createElement('a');
    a.href = router.buildUrl(path, queryParams);
    a.textContent = text;
    
    a.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
        e.preventDefault();
        router.navigate(path, { queryParams });
    });
    
    return a;
}

/**
 * Create a back button
 */
export function createBackButton(fallbackPath = '/') {
    const btn = document.createElement('button');
    btn.className = 'back-btn';
    btn.innerHTML = '← Back';
    
    btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        if (history.length > 1) {
            router.back();
        } else {
            router.navigate(fallbackPath);
        }
    });
    
    return btn;
}
```

## State Persistence

### Session State Manager
```javascript
// js/state.js
class StateManager {
    constructor() {
        this.state = this.loadFromHash() || this.getDefaults();
    }
    
    getDefaults() {
        return {
            searchQuery: '',
            selectedConversation: null,
            scrollPosition: 0,
            filters: {
                agents: [],
                dateRange: null
            },
            view: 'list'
        };
    }
    
    /**
     * Encode state in URL hash
     */
    encodeState() {
        const path = this.getPathFromState();
        const queryParams = this.getQueryParamsFromState();
        return router.buildUrl(path, queryParams);
    }
    
    /**
     * Restore state from current URL
     */
    loadFromHash() {
        const hash = window.location.hash;
        if (!hash || hash === '#' || hash === '#/') {
            return null;
        }
        
        // State is implicitly stored in route params
        // Router will call appropriate handlers
        return null;
    }
    
    /**
     * Update URL to reflect current state
     */
    syncToUrl(replace = false) {
        const path = this.getPathFromState();
        const queryParams = this.getQueryParamsFromState();
        router.navigate(path, { replace, queryParams });
    }
    
    getPathFromState() {
        if (this.state.selectedConversation) {
            return `/c/${this.state.selectedConversation}`;
        }
        if (this.state.searchQuery) {
            return '/search';
        }
        return '/';
    }
    
    getQueryParamsFromState() {
        const params = {};
        if (this.state.searchQuery) {
            params.q = this.state.searchQuery;
        }
        if (this.state.filters.agents?.length) {
            params.agent = this.state.filters.agents.join(',');
        }
        if (this.state.filters.dateRange?.from) {
            params.from = this.state.filters.dateRange.from;
        }
        if (this.state.filters.dateRange?.to) {
            params.to = this.state.filters.dateRange.to;
        }
        return params;
    }
}

export const stateManager = new StateManager();
```

## Deep Link Generation

### Share Link Creation
```javascript
// js/share.js

/**
 * Generate shareable link to current view
 */
export function getShareableLink() {
    return window.location.href;
}

/**
 * Generate link to specific conversation
 */
export function getConversationLink(conversationId, options = {}) {
    const base = window.location.origin + window.location.pathname;
    const path = `/c/${encodeURIComponent(conversationId)}`;
    
    const params = {};
    if (options.messageId) {
        return `${base}#/c/${conversationId}/m/${options.messageId}`;
    }
    if (options.highlight) {
        params.highlight = options.highlight;
    }
    
    return base + router.buildUrl(path, params);
}

/**
 * Generate link to search results
 */
export function getSearchLink(query, filters = {}) {
    const base = window.location.origin + window.location.pathname;
    const params = { q: query, ...filters };
    return base + router.buildUrl('/search', params);
}

/**
 * Copy link to clipboard with feedback
 */
export async function copyLinkToClipboard(link) {
    try {
        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(link);
        showToast('Link copied to clipboard');
        return true;
    } catch (err) {
        // Fallback for older browsers
        const textarea = document.createElement('textarea');
        textarea.value = link;
        document.body.appendChild(textarea);
        textarea.select();
        document.execCommand('copy');
        document.body.removeChild(textarea);
        showToast('Link copied to clipboard');
        return true;
    }
}
```

## Browser History Integration

### History State Management
```javascript
// Enhanced router with state preservation
class EnhancedRouter extends HashRouter {
    navigate(path, options = {}) {
        const state = {
            path,
            timestamp: Date.now(),
            scrollY: window.scrollY,
            ...options.state
        };
        
        const queryString = options.queryParams 
            ? '?' + new URLSearchParams(options.queryParams).toString()
            : '';
        
        const hash = `#${path}${queryString}`;
        
        if (options.replace) {
            history.replaceState(state, '', hash);
        } else {
            history.pushState(state, '', hash);
        }
        
        this.handleRoute();
    }
    
    handleRoute() {
        super.handleRoute();
        
        // Restore scroll position if available
        const state = history.state;
        if (state?.scrollY !== undefined) {
            requestAnimationFrame(() => {
                window.scrollTo(0, state.scrollY);
            });
        }
    }
}
```

## Pre-Auth Route Handling

### Deferred Deep Links
```javascript
// js/auth.js

// Store intended destination before auth
let pendingRoute = null;

/**
 * Check if there's a deep link and store it for post-auth
 */
export function capturePendingRoute() {
    const hash = window.location.hash;
    if (hash && hash !== '#/' && hash !== '#') {
        pendingRoute = hash;
        // Don't clear the hash - it helps users know where they'll land
    }
}

/**
 * Navigate to stored route after successful auth
 */
export function navigateToPendingRoute() {
    if (pendingRoute) {
        window.location.hash = pendingRoute;
        pendingRoute = null;
    } else {
        router.navigate('/');
    }
}

// On app init, before auth
capturePendingRoute();

// After successful unlock
onAuthSuccess(() => {
    navigateToPendingRoute();
});
```

- [ ] Hash-based router handles all defined route patterns
- [ ] Back/forward browser buttons work correctly
- [ ] Deep links to conversations load correctly after auth
- [ ] Search queries persist in URL
- [ ] Share links can be copied and work when opened
- [ ] Scroll position restored on back navigation
- [ ] No page reloads during navigation
- [ ] Works with Service Worker (offline cache)
